examples.sentence: 彼が嘘つきだとすれば、その話は信じられない。
examples.translation: If he’s a liar, then his story can’t be believed.
examples.translationEn: If he’s a liar, then his story can’t be believed.
examples.romaji: Kare ga usotsuki da to sureba, sono hanashi wa shinjirarenai.

examples.sentence: あなたが一緒に行くとすれば、私も行きます。
examples.translation: If you are going, I'll go too.
examples.translationEn: If you are going, I'll go too.
examples.romaji: Anata ga issho ni iku to sureba, watashi mo ikimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼が浮気しているとすれば、どうすればいい？
examples.translation: If he’s cheating, what should I do?
examples.translationEn: If he’s cheating, what should I do?
examples.romaji: Kare ga uwaki shite iru to sureba, dou sureba ii?

examples.sentence: その薬が効かないとすれば、他の方法を試すべきだ。
examples.translation: If that medicine doesn’t work, we should try something else.
examples.translationEn: If that medicine doesn’t work, we should try something else.
examples.romaji: Sono kusuri ga kikanai to sureba, hoka no houhou o tamesu beki da.

examples.sentence: 旅行に行くとなったら、荷造りをしなければいけません。
examples.translation: If it comes to going on a trip, we have to pack.
examples.translationEn: If it comes to going on a trip, we have to pack.
examples.romaji: Ryokou ni iku to nattara, nizukuri o shinakereba ikemasen.

examples.sentence: 試験に落ちるとなったら、どうしよう。
examples.translation: What should I do if I fail the exam?
examples.translationEn: What should I do if I fail the exam?
examples.romaji: Shiken ni ochiru to nattara, dou shiyou.

examples.sentence: 彼女と別れるとなったら、とても悲しいです。
examples.translation: If it comes to breaking up with her, I’ll be very sad.
examples.translationEn: If it comes to breaking up with her, I’ll be very sad.
examples.romaji: Kanojo to wakareru to nattara, totemo kanashii desu.

examples.sentence: 結婚するとなったら、人生が大きく変わるでしょう。
examples.translation: When it comes to getting married, life will probably change drastically.
examples.translationEn: When it comes to getting married, life will probably change drastically.
examples.romaji: Kekkon suru to nattara, jinsei ga ookiku kawaru deshou.

examples.sentence: 試験となると、多くの学生が緊張する。
examples.translation: When it comes to exams, many students get nervous.
examples.translationEn: When it comes to exams, many students get nervous.
examples.romaji: Shiken to naru to, ooku no gakusei ga kinchou suru.

examples.sentence: 東京へ引っ越すとなると、生活費が一気に上がる。
examples.translation: If you move to Tokyo, the cost of living goes up drastically.
examples.translationEn: If you move to Tokyo, the cost of living goes up drastically.
examples.romaji: Toukyou e hikkosu to naru to, seikatsuhi ga ikki ni agaru.

examples.sentence: 締め切りが明日となると、今夜は徹夜になりそうだ。
examples.translation: If the deadline is tomorrow, I’ll probably be up all night.
examples.translationEn: If the deadline is tomorrow, I’ll probably be up all night.
examples.romaji: Shimekiri ga ashita to naru to, konya wa tetsuya ni narisou da.

examples.sentence: 彼がリーダーとなると、チームの活動は大きく改善されるかもしれない。
examples.translation: If he becomes the leader, the team’s activities might improve significantly.
examples.translationEn: If he becomes the leader, the team’s activities might improve significantly.
examples.romaji: Kare ga riidaa to naru to, chiimu no katsudou wa ookiku kaizen sareru kamoshirenai.

examples.sentence: 彼がリーダーとなれば、プロジェクトは成功するでしょう。
examples.translation: If he becomes the leader, the project will probably succeed.
examples.translationEn: If he becomes the leader, the project will probably succeed.
examples.romaji: Kare ga riidaa to nareba, purojekuto wa seikou suru deshou.

examples.sentence: 明日が雨だとなれば、ピクニックをキャンセルしましょう。
examples.translation: If it turns out to be raining tomorrow, let's cancel the picnic.
examples.translationEn: If it turns out to be raining tomorrow, let's cancel the picnic.
examples.romaji: Ashita ga ame da to nareba, pikunikku wo kyanseru shimashou.

examples.sentence: この仕事が終わったとなれば、休暇を取ることができます。
examples.translation: If this job is over, you can take a vacation.
examples.translationEn: If this job is over, you can take a vacation.
examples.romaji: Kono shigoto ga owatta to nareba, kyuuka wo toru koto ga dekimasu.

examples.sentence: そのテストが難しいとなれば、より一層勉強しなければならない。
examples.translation: If that test is difficult, we must study all the more.
examples.translationEn: If that test is difficult, we must study all the more.
examples.romaji: Sono tesuto ga muzukashii to nareba, yori issou benkyou shinakereba naranai.

examples.sentence: 週末にパーティーがあるとのことだ。
examples.translation: I've heard that there is a party this weekend.
examples.translationEn: I've heard that there is a party this weekend.
examples.romaji: Shuumatsu ni paatii ga aru to no koto da.

examples.sentence: 彼は来年ロンドンに引っ越すとのことだ。
examples.translation: I was told that he is moving to London next year.
examples.translationEn: I was told that he is moving to London next year.
examples.romaji: Kare wa rainen Rondon ni hikkosu to no koto da.

examples.sentence: 彼女は新しい仕事についたとのことだ。
examples.translation: I've heard that she has gotten a new job.
examples.translationEn: I've heard that she has gotten a new job.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa atarashii shigoto ni tsuita to no koto da.

examples.sentence: 明日は試験がないとのことだ。
examples.translation: I was told that there is no exam tomorrow.
examples.translationEn: I was told that there is no exam tomorrow.
examples.romaji: Ashita wa shiken ga nai to no koto da.

examples.sentence: 彼は頭が良いとはいえ、少し怠け者だ。
examples.translation: Although he is smart, he is a bit lazy.
examples.translationEn: Although he is smart, he is a bit lazy.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atama ga yoi to wa ie, sukoshi namakemono da.

examples.sentence: このレストランは美味しいとはいえ、ちょっと高い。
examples.translation: While this restaurant is delicious, it's a bit expensive.
examples.translationEn: While this restaurant is delicious, it's a bit expensive.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa oishii to wa ie, chotto takai.

examples.sentence: 日本は小さい国だとはいえ、技術は世界一だ。
examples.translation: Even though Japan is a small country, its technology is among the best in the world.
examples.translationEn: Even though Japan is a small country, its technology is among the best in the world.
examples.romaji: Nihon wa chiisai kuni da to wa ie, gijutsu wa sekaiichi da.

examples.sentence: 彼女は若いとはいえ、経験豊富だ。
examples.translation: Even though she is young, she is very experienced.
examples.translationEn: Even though she is young, she is very experienced.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa wakai to wa ie, keiken houfu da.

examples.sentence: 彼は運動が得意とみえて、毎日ジムに行っています。
examples.translation: He seems to be good at exercise, as he goes to the gym every day.
examples.translationEn: He seems to be good at exercise, as he goes to the gym every day.
examples.romaji: Kare wa undou ga tokui to miete, mainichi jimu ni itteimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は忙しいとみえて、なかなか会えない。
examples.translation: She seems to be busy, as it's hard to meet her.
examples.translationEn: She seems to be busy, as it's hard to meet her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa isogashii to miete, nakanaka aenai.

examples.sentence: 田中さんは疲れているとみえて、早く帰りました。
examples.translation: It seems that Tanaka-san was tired, as he went home early.
examples.translationEn: It seems that Tanaka-san was tired, as he went home early.
examples.romaji: Tanaka-san wa tsukarete iru to miete, hayaku kaerimashita.

examples.sentence: その店は人気だとみえて、いつも行列ができている。
examples.translation: That shop seems to be popular, as there is always a line.
examples.translationEn: That shop seems to be popular, as there is always a line.
examples.romaji: Sono mise wa ninki da to miete, itsumo retsu ga dekite iru.

examples.sentence: 彼の行動は無責任とみられる。
examples.translation: His behavior is considered irresponsible.
examples.translationEn: His behavior is considered irresponsible.
examples.romaji: Kare no koudou wa musekinin to mirareru.

examples.sentence: この絵はピカソの作品とみられている。
examples.translation: This painting is believed to be a work of Picasso.
examples.translationEn: This painting is believed to be a work of Picasso.
examples.romaji: Kono e wa pikaso no sakuhin to mirareteiru.

examples.sentence: 地震の後、被害はさらに増加するとみられる。
examples.translation: After the earthquake, damage is expected to increase further.
examples.translationEn: After the earthquake, damage is expected to increase further.
examples.romaji: Jishin no ato, higai wa sara ni zouka suru to mirareru.

examples.sentence: 週末は天気が良くなるとみられる。
examples.translation: The weather is expected to improve on the weekend.
examples.translationEn: The weather is expected to improve on the weekend.
examples.romaji: Shuumatsu wa tenki ga yoku naru to mirareru.

examples.sentence: この問題を数学の視点でとみると、解決策が見えてきます。
examples.translation: When one sees this problem from a mathematical perspective, a solution begins to emerge.
examples.translationEn: When one sees this problem from a mathematical perspective, a solution begins to emerge.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai o suugaku no shiten de to miru to, kaiketsusaku ga miete kimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女が優秀な学生だとみると、その賞をもらったのは当然です。
examples.translation: Considering she is an excellent student, it's natural that she received that award.
examples.translationEn: Considering she is an excellent student, it's natural that she received that award.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga yuushuuna gakusei da to miru to, sono shou o moratta no wa touzen desu.

examples.sentence: 彼が新入社員だとみると、その成果は素晴らしいです。
examples.translation: Viewing him as a new employee, his achievements are exceptional.
examples.translationEn: Viewing him as a new employee, his achievements are exceptional.
examples.romaji: Kare ga shinnyuushain da to miru to, sono seika wa subarashii desu.

examples.sentence: このパンダが絶滅危惧種だとみると、保護が必要です。
examples.translation: Considering this panda is an endangered species, it needs protection.
examples.translationEn: Considering this panda is an endangered species, it needs protection.
examples.romaji: Kono panda ga zetsumetsu kigushu da to miru to, hogo ga hitsuyou desu.

examples.sentence: どんなに頑張ろうが、彼の速さには追いつけない。
examples.translation: No matter how hard I try, I can't catch up with his speed.
examples.translationEn: No matter how hard I try, I can't catch up with his speed.
examples.romaji: Donna ni ganbarou ga, kare no hayasa ni wa oitsukenai.

examples.sentence: どんなに食べようが、彼女は太らない。
examples.translation: No matter how much she eats, she doesn't gain weight.
examples.translationEn: No matter how much she eats, she doesn't gain weight.
examples.romaji: Donna ni tabeyou ga, kanojo wa futoranai.

examples.sentence: どんなに寒かろうが、彼は毎日ジョギングをします。
examples.translation: No matter how cold it is, he goes jogging every day.
examples.translationEn: No matter how cold it is, he goes jogging every day.
examples.romaji: Donna ni samukarou ga, kare wa mainichi jogging wo shimasu.

examples.sentence: どんなに暑かろうが、エアコンは使いません。
examples.translation: No matter how hot it is, I don't use air conditioning.
examples.translationEn: No matter how hot it is, I don't use air conditioning.
examples.romaji: Donna ni atsukarou ga, eakon wa tsukaimasen.
